name: managing-git
description: Manages Git workflows including branching, commits, and pull requests. Use when working with Git, creating commits, opening PRs, managing branches, resolving conflicts, or when asked about version control best practices.
capabilities: Provides GitHub Flow and Git Flow branching strategy diagrams and guidance
Defines Conventional Commits format with type definitions and examples
Offers branch naming conventions with ticket-based patterns
Includes pull request checklists and size guidelines
Shares common Git commands for daily workflows and mistake fixes
Lists commit validation criteria before pushing
author: CloudAI-X
securitySummary: This skill is pure Markdown documentation containing Git workflow guidance, checklists, and command examples. No executable code, scripts, network calls, or filesystem access detected. All static findings are false positives triggered by documentation patterns (code blocks, commit message examples, and security-related terminology).
valueStatement: Developers often struggle with inconsistent Git practices leading to merge conflicts and confusing commit histories. This skill provides clear guidelines for branching strategies, commit conventions, and pull request workflows to maintain clean and collaborative repositories.
limitations: Does not execute Git commands directly on user repositories
Does not integrate with CI/CD systems or Git hosting APIs
Does not resolve merge conflicts automatically
Does not enforce commit policies through hooks or server-side rules

useCases.title: Learn clean commit habits
useCases.description: Adopt Conventional Commits and branch naming for clearer project history

useCases.title: Standardize team workflows
useCases.description: Establish consistent branching and PR processes across development teams

useCases.title: Guide contributor submissions
useCases.description: Provide PR templates and checklist workflows for external contributors

promptTemplates.title: Basic commit help
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a properly formatted commit message
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me write a Conventional Commit message for adding user authentication to my project

promptTemplates.title: Branch strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: Should I use GitHub Flow or Git Flow for my SaaS application that releases monthly?

promptTemplates.title: PR template
promptTemplates.scenario: Setting up a pull request template
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a pull request template checklist for my team that covers testing, security, and documentation

promptTemplates.title: Fix git mistakes
promptTemplates.prompt: I accidentally committed sensitive data and pushed it. How do I safely remove it from history?

bestPractices: Make atomic commits with one logical change per commit to simplify reviews and rollbacks
Use descriptive commit messages that explain the 'why' behind changes, not just the 'what'
Rebase on latest main before creating PRs to keep history linear and reduce merge conflicts
antiPatterns: Avoid WIP commits like 'fix stuff' or 'updates' that lack context and purpose
Do not commit directly to main branch; always use feature branches with PR review
Avoid large PRs over 500 lines that overwhelm reviewers; split into smaller pieces

faq.question: What Git hosting platforms are supported?
faq.answer: This skill works with GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and any Git-based repository. Commands and workflows are platform-agnostic.

faq.question: What are the size limits for pull requests?
faq.answer: Under 50 lines is XS (quick review), 50-200 is S (standard), 200-500 is M (thorough), and 500+ should be split when possible.

faq.question: Does this skill integrate with Git hosting APIs?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides guidance and templates only. It does not execute API calls or integrate directly with GitHub, GitLab, or other platforms.

faq.question: Is my repository data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill contains only documentation and templates. It does not read, write, or modify any repository files or Git data.

faq.question: Why are my commits showing merge conflicts?
faq.answer: Conflicts occur when multiple branches modify the same lines. Rebase your branch on latest main before creating PRs and stage changes interactively with git add -p.

faq.question: How does this compare to Git auto-merge tools?
faq.answer: This skill teaches Git concepts and best practices rather than automating merges. It helps users understand when to use rebase, cherry-pick, or merge strategies.
